proved: The repository contains a complete worker/supervisor protocol, a fill-in goal template, and a reusable supervisor heartbeat prompt.

The structural tests fail if the runbook loses its two-check recovery trigger, last-verified-checkpoint rule, paused-user behavior, unattended-permission behavior, quiet notification policy, or supervisor cleanup rule.
The goal template requires objective, non-goals, authority boundary, falsifiable gates, checkpoint evidence, per-operation deadlines, recovery state, and completion cleanup.

The supervisor template is read-only with respect to repository work, targets only the stale operation, preserves unrelated work, skips unattended permission waits, and deletes itself after completion.
The framework test binds its timing claims to the executable watchdog's 30-second default and 60-second maximum heartbeat.

The completed Syzygy engine-shutdown slice used bounded commands, focused checkpoints, headless tests, packaged process/DLL proof, machine-readable evidence, a pushed commit, and removal of the obsolete supervisor.
notProved: A scheduler or model will always deliver a heartbeat on time under host suspension, network loss, application termination, or service outage.

The supervisor has exercised every recovery branch; stale-operation termination, unattended permission skipping, repeated-block classification, and cross-platform process inspection need dedicated injected end-to-end fixtures.
A Git checkpoint makes incorrect work correct; it only provides a bounded, inspectable recovery point whose evidence must still be challenged.
The suggested operation deadlines fit every machine or future repository; they are initial ceilings that must be revised from measured evidence.
